steps.title: Set up the sine ratio
steps.content:
From the figure, the side opposite angle $A$ has length $35$ and the hypotenuse has length $171$. By SOH-CAH-TOA:
$$\sin A = \frac{\text{opposite}}{\text{hypotenuse}}$$

steps.title: Compute
steps.content:
Substitute the two lengths:
$$\sin A = \frac{35}{171}$$
That matches choice B — B is correct.
